# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Docker Compose configuration for a MongoDB development environment. Define MongoDB service with exposed ports for application connectivity. Set up volume mounts for data persistence. This is for our local development and testing setup.
# Category: mongodb_exposed_port

version: '3'  # using Docker Compose version v3
services:     # define services here, each service has one or more containers in it that are defined by the YAML file under its section name (e.g., mongoDBService) which can have multiple volumes and exposed ports for accessibility from outside world   e.g 'mongo1' is an instance of Mongodb
  db:         # create a service named `db` that represents database as container running in the docker compose file (it contains all data required to run your app) it will be linked with mongoClientService and exposed on port 27018 for development purposes. It's better not expose services ports publicly, instead use them internally if you have a specific need
    image: mongodb          # specify the Docker Image of MongodDB service here (it should be official version)   e.g., latest and other names will also work but they would point to different versions in dockerhub or quay etc as their tags are private/secure so you'd need a token for access
    ports:                  # set exposed port mapping from the Docker container internally (27018) into host machine. If it is not required, leave this empty which defaults to exposing all interfaces on 27017 by default in docker-compose v3/v4 or leaving out entire section if you don't need any public access
      - "27018:27017"       # it maps port from container internally and hosts machine to external ports [default] (e.g., hostPort :containerPort), use same format for multiple exposing, separated by commas  e.g.- mongoClientService
    volumes:                # mounts the data volume inside Docker containers which stores your MongodDB'data across container restarts and can be shared with other services running in docker-compose file (like mongoclient) or any host machine from where you want to access that persistent storage. This will not expose ports publicly, use them internally if needed
      - ./mongoData:/data/db    # this is mapping the directory containing data on Host Machine and /data/db inside Docker container at location :./Mongodata (default) so Mapped folder can be persistent across restarts. If it's not required, leave out entire section if you don’t need any public access
